mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing present values of future cash flows and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) across 100+ rate decline observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market health and reduced consumer spending capacity, which suppresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.4pp threshold, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio remains negative.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand recovery, which accelerates revenue growth across cyclical sectors and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a classification accuracy above 52%, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals capacity constraints and impending supply bottlenecks, which trigger short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle phases are followed by positive 5-day returns in over 60% of cases, or the predictive signal reverses direction when GDPC1 growth exceeds 3% annually.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines while CPI inflation simultaneously decelerates, real borrowing costs fall sharply, boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal of falling FEDFUNDS and falling CPIAUCSL yields a negative or zero 20-day forward return in more than 50% of historical occurrences, or the interaction coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate household income and consumer purchasing power, which directly supports corporate revenue expansion and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth fails to correlate with positive 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.01) across multiple business cycles, or the signal loses predictive power when GDPC1 contracts.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction exceeding 0.5% confirms an economic recession, which immediately triggers margin compression and earnings downgrades, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts positive 5-day returns in more than 45% of quarters where GDPC1 declines by over 0.5%, or the signal shows no statistical edge in out-of-sample backtests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside falling unemployment during a post-recession recovery phase indicates improving capacity utilization and labor market tightening, which supports earnings upgrades and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combination of rising INDPRO and falling UNRATE during recovery phases fails to predict positive 20-day returns with a classification accuracy above 53%, or the signal breaks down when FEDFUNDS exceeds 2%.
